name: Joe Cooper's Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. In a six-quart pot, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and cook until browned but no longer pink, stirring constantly.
2. Drain the meat and add water to cover the bottom of the pot by about an inch. Add bay leaves.
3. Simmer the mixture for 15 minutes, then remove and discard the bay leaves.
4. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer the chili with the remaining ingredients (salt, garlic, cumin, oregano, cayenne, black pepper, sugar, paprika) until well combined.
5. Stir in cocoa if using. Continue cooking for 30 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent sticking.
6. Remove any excess fat that rises to the top.
7. Blend flour with cold water to create a paste, then stir it into the chili along with cornmeal. Cook, stirring constantly, until the desired consistency is reached.
Note: Adjust seasonings as needed before adding the flour and cornmeal mixture.
